prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `15663ba9`.
codex_response:
Implemented `15663ba9` in the new task package at [__init__.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_15663ba9/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_15663ba9/verifier.py#L6), [generator.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_15663ba9/generator.py#L32), [helpers.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_15663ba9/helpers.py#L4), and [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_15663ba9/notes.md#L1).

Rule summary: each recolored cell is an L-turn on a 1-cell-wide orthogonal cycle. It becomes `4` when the turn’s open-side diagonal reaches exterior background, and `2` when that diagonal faces an enclosed pocket. I used the `opus` hint as a starting point and discarded the `sonnet` hint; the mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_15663ba9/notes.md#L1). No new primitive was added to `arc2/dsl_ext.py`.

The generator matches the task family by placing 2-3 disjoint single-color orthogonal loops on black backgrounds, mixing plain rectangles with inward-notched rectangles so the outputs contain the same convex/concave corner markings as the official examples. Artifacts were built at [task.json](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/15663ba9/task.json), [originals.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/15663ba9/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/15663ba9/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/15663ba9/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/15663ba9/previews/preview_03.png).

Validation passed end to end: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`. I also did a terminal-rendered sanity check of `preview_01.png`; the generated sheets stay on-family visually.
